seoTitle: How to Get a HubSpot API Key (Private App Method)
metaDescription: HubSpot retired legacy API keys, so learn how to create a Private App, assign scopes, and reveal your secure bearer token for API access instead.
intro: HubSpot has phased out traditional API keys in favor of Private Apps with scoped, token-based access, so if you're looking for an old-style key you'll need this newer path instead. This guide covers creating a Private App, choosing the right read and write scopes, and revealing your bearer token securely. It also touches on monitoring your integration through HubSpot's call logs.

steps.heading: Open Integrations settings
steps.body: Go to your HubSpot account settings and navigate to the Integrations or Development dashboard.

steps.heading: Create a Private App
steps.body: Click Create Private App to start a fresh integration profile for your project.

steps.heading: Fill in app details
steps.body: Add an app name, logo, and functional description so the integration is easy to identify later.

steps.heading: Choose your scopes
steps.body: Go to the Scopes tab and check off the exact read and write permissions you need for Contacts, Companies, or Deals.

steps.heading: Create the app and reveal your token
steps.body: Click Create App, then open the Auth tab and click Reveal to display your bearer access token.

steps.heading: Test and monitor your integration
steps.body: Copy the token, run a test call, and use the integration dashboard to audit call history and sync status.

faq.question: Does HubSpot still support the old API key system?
faq.answer: No, legacy API keys have been deprecated in favor of Private Apps and OAuth-based authentication.

faq.question: What is the difference between a Private App and OAuth?
faq.answer: A Private App is scoped to a single account and simpler to set up, while OAuth apps support multi-account, multi-user access.

faq.question: Can I limit what data my API token can access?
faq.answer: Yes, the Scopes tab lets you restrict access to specific objects and whether they're read-only or read-write.

faq.question: Where can I check my API call history?
faq.answer: The integration dashboard includes logs you can use to audit call histories and confirm sync activity.
